celebration.name: Mary, Mother of God
celebration.quote: My soul proclaims the greatness of the Lord.
celebration.description: The Solemnity of Mary, the Holy Mother of God, celebrates Mary's divine motherhood and her role in salvation history.

celebration.name: Basil the Great and Gregory Nazianzen
celebration.quote: We must prefer nothing whatever to Christ.
celebration.description: These two fourth-century bishops and theologians were close friends who defended the divinity of Christ against the Arian heresy.

celebration.name: Most Holy Name of Jesus
celebration.quote: At the name of Jesus every knee should bow.
celebration.description: This feast honors the name of Jesus, which means "God saves," reminding us of the power in Christ's holy name.

celebration.name: Elizabeth Ann Seton
celebration.quote: The first end I propose in our daily work is to do the will of God.
celebration.description: First native-born American saint, she founded the first American religious community for women and Catholic schools in America.

celebration.name: John Neumann
celebration.quote: A man must always be ready, for death comes when and where God wills it.
celebration.description: Fourth Bishop of Philadelphia, he established the first Catholic diocesan school system in the United States.

celebration.name: Epiphany of the Lord
celebration.quote: We have seen his star at its rising and have come to do him homage.
celebration.description: The Epiphany celebrates the manifestation of Christ to the Gentiles, represented by the Magi.

celebration.name: Raymond of Penyafort
celebration.quote: The law of the Church is the law of love.
celebration.description: A Dominican priest who compiled the Decretals of Gregory IX and was a patron of canon lawyers.

celebration.name: Hilary of Poitiers
celebration.quote: Everything that seems empty is full of the angels of God.
celebration.description: Bishop and Doctor of the Church who defended orthodox Christianity against Arianism in the West.

celebration.name: Anthony, Abbot
celebration.quote: Our life and our death is with our neighbor.
celebration.description: Father of Christian monasticism who spent 20 years alone in the Egyptian desert.

celebration.name: Fabian
celebration.quote: The Church grows through the blood of martyrs.
celebration.description: Pope and martyr who led the Church for 14 years during a time of peace before his martyrdom.

celebration.name: Agnes
celebration.quote: Christ is my spouse; he has chosen me.
celebration.description: Virgin martyr who died at age 12 or 13 for refusing to marry, choosing consecration to Christ.

celebration.name: Vincent
celebration.quote: You may torture this body, but you cannot touch my soul.
celebration.description: Deacon and martyr from Spain who remained faithful during Diocletian's persecution.

celebration.name: Francis de Sales
celebration.quote: Be patient with everyone, but above all with yourself.
celebration.description: Bishop of Geneva, Doctor of the Church, known for his gentleness and writings on spiritual life.

celebration.name: Conversion of Paul
celebration.quote: It is no longer I who live, but Christ who lives in me.
celebration.description: Celebrates Paul's dramatic conversion on the road to Damascus from persecutor to apostle.

celebration.name: Timothy and Titus
celebration.quote: Fight the good fight of faith.
celebration.description: Companions of St. Paul and first-generation bishops who helped establish the early Church.

celebration.name: Angela Merici
celebration.quote: Have patience and remain wide awake. God wills it.
celebration.description: Founded the Ursulines, the first teaching order of women in the Church.

celebration.name: Thomas Aquinas
celebration.quote: To one who has faith, no explanation is necessary. To one without faith, no explanation is possible.
celebration.description: Dominican priest, Doctor of the Church, greatest medieval theologian and philosopher.

celebration.name: John Bosco
celebration.quote: Run, jump, shout, but do not sin.
celebration.description: Founded the Salesians to educate poor youth, pioneering preventive system of education.

celebration.name: Presentation of the Lord
celebration.quote: Now you dismiss your servant, O Lord, according to your word in peace.
celebration.description: Commemorates the presentation of Jesus in the Temple and the purification of Mary.

celebration.name: Blaise
celebration.quote: May God deliver you from every evil of the throat and from every other evil.
celebration.description: Bishop and martyr, patron of throat ailments, traditionally invoked for blessing of throats.

celebration.name: Agatha
celebration.quote: Jesus Christ, Lord of all, you see my heart.
celebration.description: Virgin martyr from Sicily who suffered torture rather than renounce her faith.

celebration.name: Paul Miki and Companions
celebration.quote: I give thanks to God that I can die as a Christian.
celebration.description: Twenty-six martyrs crucified in Nagasaki, Japan, including Jesuits, Franciscans, and laypeople.

celebration.name: Jerome Emiliani
celebration.quote: Jesus Christ, our teacher and guide, you are the way, the truth, and the life.
celebration.description: Founded the Somaschi Fathers to care for orphans and the education of youth.

celebration.name: Scholastica
celebration.quote: He can do more whose love is greater.
celebration.description: Twin sister of St. Benedict, foundress of Benedictine nuns.

celebration.name: Our Lady of Lourdes
celebration.quote: I am the Immaculate Conception.
celebration.description: Commemorates the apparitions of Mary to Bernadette Soubirous at Lourdes, France.

celebration.name: Cyril and Methodius
celebration.quote: Go therefore and make disciples of all nations.
celebration.description: Brothers who evangelized the Slavic peoples and created the Cyrillic alphabet.

celebration.name: Seven Founders of the Servite Order
celebration.quote: We are servants of Mary, the Mother of God.
celebration.description: Seven Florentine merchants who withdrew to serve Mary and founded the Servites.

celebration.name: Peter Damian
celebration.quote: The hermitage is a school of heavenly doctrine.
celebration.description: Cardinal, Doctor of the Church, reformer of Church discipline and clerical life.

celebration.name: Chair of Peter
celebration.quote: You are Peter, and upon this rock I will build my Church.
celebration.description: Celebrates the authority Christ gave to Peter and his successors.

celebration.name: Polycarp
celebration.quote: Eighty-six years I have served Him, and He has done me no wrong.
celebration.description: Bishop of Smyrna, disciple of John the Apostle, martyred at age 86.

celebration.name: Casimir
celebration.quote: I would rather die than offend God by sin.
celebration.description: Polish prince who renounced the throne to live a life of celibacy and devotion.

celebration.name: Perpetua and Felicity
celebration.quote: Stand fast in the faith.
celebration.description: Noble woman and her slave, martyred together in Carthage, leaving a powerful diary.

celebration.name: John of God
celebration.quote: Work hard, brothers, for love of God.
celebration.description: Founded the Brothers Hospitallers to care for the sick and poor.

celebration.name: Frances of Rome
celebration.quote: A married woman must leave God at the altar to find Him in her housekeeping.
celebration.description: Married woman who founded a religious community while caring for her family.

celebration.name: Patrick
celebration.quote: Christ with me, Christ before me, Christ behind me.
celebration.description: Patron of Ireland who evangelized the Irish people in the 5th century.

celebration.name: Cyril of Jerusalem
celebration.quote: The dragon sits by the side of the road, watching those who pass.
celebration.description: Bishop and Doctor of the Church, known for his catechetical instructions.

celebration.name: Joseph
celebration.quote: Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife.
celebration.description: Foster father of Jesus, patron of the universal Church and workers.

celebration.name: Turibius of Mogrovejo
celebration.quote: Christ is my life and death is my gain.
celebration.description: Archbishop of Lima who reformed the Church in Peru and defended indigenous peoples.

celebration.name: Annunciation
celebration.quote: Let it be done unto me according to your word.
celebration.description: Celebrates the angel Gabriel's announcement to Mary and the Incarnation.

celebration.name: Francis of Paola
celebration.quote: Charity is the most excellent way to God.
celebration.description: Hermit and founder of the Minims, known for humility and miracles.

celebration.name: Isidore of Seville
celebration.quote: Learning unsupported by grace may get into our ears; it never reaches the heart.
celebration.description: Bishop and Doctor of the Church, last of the Latin Fathers.

celebration.name: Vincent Ferrer
celebration.quote: Fear not! Tomorrow Christ will come.
celebration.description: Dominican preacher who helped end the Western Schism.

celebration.name: John Baptist de la Salle
celebration.quote: Remember you are in the holy presence of God.
celebration.description: Founded the Christian Brothers and pioneered education for the poor.

celebration.name: Stanislaus
celebration.quote: It is better to suffer for justice than to reign by injustice.
celebration.description: Bishop of Krakow martyred by the king for defending Church rights.

celebration.name: Martin I
celebration.quote: May God forgive them, for they know not what they do.
celebration.description: Pope and martyr who defended orthodox Christology against Monothelitism.

celebration.name: Anselm
celebration.quote: God is that than which nothing greater can be conceived.
celebration.description: Archbishop of Canterbury, Doctor of the Church, father of Scholasticism.

celebration.name: George
celebration.quote: I am a soldier of Christ; I cannot fight.
celebration.description: Soldier martyr, patron of England, symbol of Christian chivalry.

celebration.name: Fidelis of Sigmaringen
celebration.quote: Woe is me if I do not preach the Gospel!
celebration.description: Capuchin friar and martyr during the Counter-Reformation.

celebration.name: Mark
celebration.quote: Go into all the world and preach the Gospel to every creature.
celebration.description: Evangelist, companion of Peter and Paul, author of the second Gospel.

celebration.name: Peter Chanel
celebration.quote: You may kill me, but my religion will not die.
celebration.description: First martyr of Oceania, missionary to Futuna Island.

celebration.name: Catherine of Siena
celebration.quote: Be who God meant you to be and you will set the world on fire.
celebration.description: Doctor of the Church, mystic who helped return the papacy to Rome.

celebration.name: Pius V
celebration.quote: Lord, increase my sufferings and my love for You.
celebration.description: Pope who implemented the Council of Trent and reformed the liturgy.

celebration.name: Joseph the Worker
celebration.quote: Work is a blessing from God.
celebration.description: Honors St. Joseph as patron of workers and the dignity of human labor.

celebration.name: Athanasius
celebration.quote: God became man so that man might become God.
celebration.description: Bishop of Alexandria, Doctor of the Church, defender of Christ's divinity.

celebration.name: Philip and James
celebration.quote: Come and see!
celebration.description: Two of the twelve apostles who spread the Gospel.

celebration.name: John of Avila
celebration.quote: We must ask God to give us what we need to serve Him.
celebration.description: Mystic, preacher, spiritual director to Teresa of Avila and John of the Cross.

celebration.name: Nereus and Achilleus
celebration.quote: We are soldiers of Christ, we cannot shed blood.
celebration.description: Roman soldiers who converted and were martyred for the faith.

celebration.name: Our Lady of Fatima
celebration.quote: Pray the Rosary every day to obtain peace for the world.
celebration.description: Commemorates Mary's apparitions to three shepherd children in Portugal.

celebration.name: Matthias
celebration.quote: You did not choose me, but I chose you.
celebration.description: Chosen to replace Judas Iscariot among the twelve apostles.

celebration.name: John I
celebration.quote: Better to die for the truth than to live in error.
celebration.description: Pope and martyr who died in prison under the Arian king Theodoric.

celebration.name: Bernardine of Siena
celebration.quote: The name of Jesus is the glory of preachers.
celebration.description: Franciscan preacher who promoted devotion to the Holy Name of Jesus.

celebration.name: Christopher Magallanes
celebration.quote: I die innocent, and ask God that my blood may serve to bring peace to our divided Mexico.
celebration.description: Mexican priest martyred with companions during the Cristero War.

celebration.name: Rita of Cascia
celebration.quote: Nothing is impossible with God.
celebration.description: Wife, mother, widow, and nun, patron of impossible causes.

celebration.name: Bede the Venerable
celebration.quote: Christ is the morning star who when the night of this world is past brings to his saints the promise of the light of life.
celebration.description: English monk, Doctor of the Church, father of English history.

celebration.name: Philip Neri
celebration.quote: Cast yourself into the arms of God and be very sure that if He wants anything of you, He will fit you for the work and give you strength.
celebration.description: Founded the Oratory, known as the "Apostle of Rome" for his joyful holiness.

celebration.name: Augustine of Canterbury
celebration.quote: Christ has sent me to you, and I bring you joyful news.
celebration.description: First Archbishop of Canterbury, evangelized the English.

celebration.name: Visitation
celebration.quote: Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb!
celebration.description: Celebrates Mary's visit to Elizabeth and the Magnificat.

celebration.name: Justin Martyr
celebration.quote: We would rather die than live a lie.
celebration.description: Philosopher who became Christian apologist, first to use philosophy to explain faith.

celebration.name: Marcellinus and Peter
celebration.quote: Christ is our strength and our crown.
celebration.description: Priest and exorcist martyred during Diocletian's persecution.

celebration.name: Charles Lwanga and Companions
celebration.quote: Tell the king that he has put us to death for nothing.
celebration.description: Ugandan martyrs who died rather than submit to the immoral demands of their king.

celebration.name: Boniface
celebration.quote: Come, brother, be of strong heart in Christ.
celebration.description: English missionary, "Apostle of Germany," martyred while evangelizing.

celebration.name: Norbert
celebration.quote: Jesus Christ, and him crucified.
celebration.description: Founded the Premonstratensians, reformed clergy, bishop of Magdeburg.

celebration.name: Ephrem
celebration.quote: Every tongue should praise Him, and every mouth should confess Him.
celebration.description: Deacon, Doctor of the Church, Syrian theologian and hymn writer.

celebration.name: Barnabas
celebration.quote: Do not lose heart. We must pass through many trials to enter the kingdom of God.
celebration.description: Companion of Paul, called an apostle though not one of the Twelve.

celebration.name: Anthony of Padua
celebration.quote: Actions speak louder than words; let your words teach and your actions speak.
celebration.description: Franciscan priest, Doctor of the Church, powerful preacher and miracle worker.

celebration.name: Romuald
celebration.quote: Sit in your cell as in paradise.
celebration.description: Founded the Camaldolese monks, combining eremitical and cenobitic life.

celebration.name: Aloysius Gonzaga
celebration.quote: It is better to be a child of God than king of the whole world.
celebration.description: Jesuit seminarian who died caring for plague victims, patron of youth.

celebration.name: Paulinus of Nola
celebration.quote: Christ is our song.
celebration.description: Senator who became bishop, known for Christian poetry.

celebration.name: Birth of John the Baptist
celebration.quote: Prepare the way of the Lord, make straight his paths.
celebration.description: Celebrates the birth of Christ's forerunner who prepared the way.

celebration.name: Cyril of Alexandria
celebration.quote: Mary is the Mother of Emmanuel, truly the Mother of God.
celebration.description: Bishop, Doctor of the Church, defended Mary as Mother of God.

celebration.name: Irenaeus
celebration.quote: The glory of God is man fully alive.
celebration.description: Bishop of Lyon, early Church Father who fought Gnosticism.

celebration.name: Peter and Paul
celebration.quote: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
celebration.description: The two great apostles, pillars of the Church, both martyred in Rome.

celebration.name: First Martyrs of Rome
celebration.quote: We would rather die than deny Christ.
celebration.description: Christians killed by Nero after the great fire of Rome in 64 AD.

celebration.name: Junipero Serra
celebration.quote: Always go forward, never turn back.
celebration.description: Franciscan missionary who founded the California missions.

celebration.name: Thomas
celebration.quote: My Lord and my God!
celebration.description: Apostle whose doubt led to profound faith, traditionally evangelized India.

celebration.name: Elizabeth of Portugal
celebration.quote: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called children of God.
celebration.description: Queen who was a peacemaker and served the poor.

celebration.name: Anthony Zaccaria
celebration.quote: Be on fire with love for Jesus Christ.
celebration.description: Founded the Barnabites to reform the clergy.

celebration.name: Maria Goretti
celebration.quote: I forgive him, and I want him to be with me in Paradise.
celebration.description: Virgin martyr who forgave her attacker, patron of youth and purity.

celebration.name: Augustine Zhao Rong and Companions
celebration.quote: It is a joyful thing to suffer for our Lord.
celebration.description: Chinese martyrs spanning three centuries of persecution.

celebration.name: Benedict
celebration.quote: Prefer nothing to the love of Christ.
celebration.description: Father of Western monasticism, wrote the Rule that shaped religious life.

celebration.name: Henry
celebration.quote: Christ must reign in our hearts and in our kingdoms.
celebration.description: Holy Roman Emperor who used power to reform the Church.

celebration.name: Kateri Tekakwitha
celebration.quote: I am not my own; I have given myself to Jesus.
celebration.description: First Native American saint, "Lily of the Mohawks."

celebration.name: Bonaventure
celebration.quote: In beautiful things he saw Beauty itself.
celebration.description: Franciscan theologian, Doctor of the Church, minister general of Franciscans.

celebration.name: Our Lady of Mount Carmel
celebration.quote: Take this scapular, whosoever dies wearing it shall not suffer eternal fire.
celebration.description: Honors Mary as patroness of Carmelites and the Brown Scapular devotion.

celebration.name: Camillus de Lellis
celebration.quote: Even if I had a thousand lives, I would dedicate them all to the service of the sick.
celebration.description: Reformed soldier who founded order to care for the sick.

celebration.name: Apollinaris
celebration.quote: Christ is my life, and death is my gain.
celebration.description: First bishop of Ravenna, martyred for the faith.

celebration.name: Lawrence of Brindisi
celebration.quote: All for the honor and glory of God.
celebration.description: Capuchin priest, Doctor of the Church, linguist and diplomat.

celebration.name: Mary Magdalene
celebration.quote: I have seen the Lord!
celebration.description: First witness to the Resurrection, apostle to the apostles.

celebration.name: Bridget
celebration.quote: Be you a bright candle before men.
celebration.description: Swedish mystic, mother of eight, founded the Brigittines.

celebration.name: Sharbel Makhluf
celebration.quote: God is found in silence and solitude.
celebration.description: Lebanese hermit monk known for miracles.

celebration.name: James
celebration.quote: Can you drink the cup that I am to drink?
celebration.description: Apostle, brother of John, first apostle to be martyred.

celebration.name: Joachim and Anne
celebration.quote: The fruit of your womb will be blessed.
celebration.description: Parents of the Blessed Virgin Mary.

celebration.name: Martha, Mary, and Lazarus
celebration.quote: Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
celebration.description: Friends of Jesus who showed hospitality and faith.

celebration.name: Peter Chrysologus
celebration.quote: All that we have is Christ's; all that Christ has, he has made ours.
celebration.description: Bishop of Ravenna, Doctor of the Church, golden-worded preacher.

celebration.name: Ignatius of Loyola
celebration.quote: Go forth and set the world on fire.
celebration.description: Founded the Jesuits, author of Spiritual Exercises.

celebration.name: Alphonsus Liguori
celebration.quote: Those who love Mary are saved; those who love her much are saints.
celebration.description: Founded Redemptorists, Doctor of the Church, moral theologian.

celebration.name: Eusebius of Vercelli
celebration.quote: Where Christ is, there is no room for heresy.
celebration.description: Bishop who fought Arianism and lived in community with his clergy.

celebration.name: John Vianney
celebration.quote: The priesthood is the love of the heart of Jesus.
celebration.description: Curé of Ars, patron of parish priests, known for confessions and holiness.

celebration.name: Dedication of St. Mary Major
celebration.quote: My house shall be called a house of prayer.
celebration.description: Commemorates the dedication of the basilica in Rome.

celebration.name: Transfiguration
celebration.quote: This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased; listen to him.
celebration.description: Celebrates Christ's divine glory revealed on Mount Tabor.

celebration.name: Cajetan
celebration.quote: Seek first the kingdom of God and his righteousness.
celebration.description: Founded the Theatines to reform clergy and serve the poor.

celebration.name: Dominic
celebration.quote: Arm yourself with prayer rather than a sword; wear humility rather than fine clothes.
celebration.description: Founded the Dominicans (Order of Preachers) to combat heresy through preaching.

celebration.name: Teresa Benedicta of the Cross
celebration.quote: The world doesn't need what women have, it needs what women are.
celebration.description: Jewish philosopher who became Carmelite nun, died at Auschwitz.

celebration.name: Lawrence
celebration.quote: Turn me over, I'm done on this side!
celebration.description: Deacon martyr who distributed Church treasures to the poor.

celebration.name: Clare
celebration.quote: We become what we love and who we love shapes what we become.
celebration.description: Founded the Poor Clares, first woman to write a religious rule.

celebration.name: Jane Frances de Chantal
celebration.quote: Jesus alone, and always Jesus.
celebration.description: Widow who founded the Visitation Sisters with Francis de Sales.

celebration.name: Pontian and Hippolytus
celebration.quote: In exile we found our unity in Christ.
celebration.description: Pope and antipope reconciled in exile and martyrdom.

celebration.name: Maximilian Kolbe
celebration.quote: No one has greater love than this, to lay down one's life for one's friends.
celebration.description: Franciscan priest who volunteered to die in place of another at Auschwitz.

celebration.name: Assumption
celebration.quote: All generations will call me blessed.
celebration.description: Celebrates Mary's assumption body and soul into heaven.

celebration.name: Stephen of Hungary
celebration.quote: I offer this kingdom to the Virgin Mary.
celebration.description: First Christian king of Hungary who established the Church there.

celebration.name: John Eudes
celebration.quote: The heart of Jesus is the heart of God made man.
celebration.description: Promoted devotion to Sacred Hearts of Jesus and Mary.

celebration.name: Bernard
celebration.quote: Mary is the neck through which the Head communicates with the body.
celebration.description: Cistercian abbot, Doctor of the Church, preached the Second Crusade.

celebration.name: Pius X
celebration.quote: Restore all things in Christ.
celebration.description: Pope who promoted frequent Communion and reformed liturgy.

celebration.name: Queenship of Mary
celebration.quote: Hail, holy Queen, Mother of Mercy.
celebration.description: Celebrates Mary as Queen of Heaven and Earth.

celebration.name: Rose of Lima
celebration.quote: Apart from the cross there is no other ladder by which we may get to heaven.
celebration.description: First saint of the Americas, Dominican tertiary and mystic.

celebration.name: Bartholomew
celebration.quote: Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are the King of Israel!
celebration.description: One of the twelve apostles, also called Nathanael, traditionally martyred in Armenia.

celebration.name: Louis
celebration.quote: I would rather be a leper than commit a mortal sin.
celebration.description: King of France who led two Crusades and cared for the poor.

celebration.name: Monica
celebration.quote: Nothing is far from God.
celebration.description: Mother of Augustine who prayed for his conversion for years.

celebration.name: Augustine
celebration.quote: You have made us for yourself, O Lord, and our hearts are restless until they rest in you.
celebration.description: Bishop of Hippo, Doctor of the Church, influential theologian and philosopher.

celebration.name: Passion of John the Baptist
celebration.quote: It is not lawful for you to have her.
celebration.description: Commemorates the martyrdom of Christ's forerunner.

celebration.name: Gregory the Great
celebration.quote: He who would climb to a lofty height must go by steps, not leaps.
celebration.description: Pope, Doctor of the Church, sent missionaries to England, reformed liturgy.

celebration.name: Birth of Mary
celebration.quote: Rejoice, O highly favored daughter! The Lord is with you.
celebration.description: Celebrates the birth of the Mother of God.

celebration.name: Peter Claver
celebration.quote: Slave of the slaves forever.
celebration.description: Jesuit who ministered to African slaves in Colombia.

celebration.name: Most Holy Name of Mary
celebration.quote: Blessed is the name of Mary.
celebration.description: Honors the name of the Blessed Virgin Mary.

celebration.name: John Chrysostom
celebration.quote: Not to enable the poor to share in our goods is to steal from them.
celebration.description: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church, "Golden-mouthed" preacher.

celebration.name: Exaltation of the Holy Cross
celebration.quote: We should glory in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ.
celebration.description: Celebrates the Cross as the instrument of our salvation.

celebration.name: Our Lady of Sorrows
celebration.quote: O all you who pass by, see if there is any sorrow like my sorrow.
celebration.description: Honors Mary's suffering as she stood by the Cross.

celebration.name: Cornelius and Cyprian
celebration.quote: Outside the Church there is no salvation.
celebration.description: Pope and bishop who were martyred during persecution.

celebration.name: Robert Bellarmine
celebration.quote: The mark of the true Church is that she suffers persecution.
celebration.description: Jesuit cardinal, Doctor of the Church, defender of the faith.

celebration.name: Januarius
celebration.quote: The blood of martyrs is the seed of Christians.
celebration.description: Bishop of Naples whose blood liquefies miraculously.

celebration.name: Andrew Kim Tae-gon and Companions
celebration.quote: This is why I was born, and why I came into the world.
celebration.description: Korean martyrs who died for the faith in the 19th century.

celebration.name: Matthew
celebration.quote: Follow me.
celebration.description: Tax collector who became apostle and evangelist.

celebration.name: Padre Pio
celebration.quote: Pray, hope, and don't worry.
celebration.description: Capuchin priest with stigmata, known for confessions and miracles.

celebration.name: Cosmas and Damian
celebration.quote: We take no payment but from God.
celebration.description: Twin brothers, physicians who treated the poor without charge.

celebration.name: Vincent de Paul
celebration.quote: Charity is the cement which binds communities to God and persons to one another.
celebration.description: Founded Vincentians and Daughters of Charity to serve the poor.

celebration.name: Wenceslaus
celebration.quote: It is better to die for God than to live without him.
celebration.description: Duke of Bohemia martyred by his brother.

celebration.name: Michael, Gabriel, and Raphael
celebration.quote: Who is like God?
celebration.description: The three archangels named in Scripture.

celebration.name: Jerome
celebration.quote: Ignorance of Scripture is ignorance of Christ.
celebration.description: Translated the Bible into Latin (Vulgate), Doctor of the Church.

celebration.name: Therese of the Child Jesus
celebration.quote: I will spend my heaven doing good on earth.
celebration.description: The Little Flower, Doctor of the Church, taught the "little way" of spiritual childhood.

celebration.name: Guardian Angels
celebration.quote: See that you do not despise one of these little ones, for their angels continually see the face of my Father.
celebration.description: Celebrates the angels God assigns to protect each person.

celebration.name: St. Thérèse of the Child Jesus (Thérèse of Lisieux)
celebration.quote: I will spend my heaven doing good on earth.
celebration.description: Known as the Little Flower, Thérèse of Lisieux was a Carmelite nun who taught the 'Little Way' of spiritual childhood. Despite dying at age 24, her autobiography 'Story of a Soul' has inspired millions. She was declared a Doctor of the Church in 1997 and is one of the patron saints of missions.

celebration.name: Francis of Assisi
celebration.quote: Preach the Gospel at all times; when necessary, use words.
celebration.description: Founded Franciscans, rebuilt the Church, patron of ecology and peace.

celebration.name: Faustina Kowalska
celebration.quote: Jesus, I trust in You!
celebration.description: Polish nun who received revelations of Divine Mercy.

celebration.name: Bruno
celebration.quote: O Bonitas! O Goodness!
celebration.description: Founded the Carthusians, strictest contemplative order.

celebration.name: Our Lady of the Rosary
celebration.quote: Hail Mary, full of grace, the Lord is with thee.
celebration.description: Commemorates the victory at Lepanto through the Rosary.

celebration.name: Denis and Companions
celebration.quote: Christ is our head, and we are his body.
celebration.description: First bishop of Paris, martyred with companions.

celebration.name: John XXIII
celebration.quote: Consult not your fears but your hopes and dreams.
celebration.description: Good Pope John who convened Vatican II.

celebration.name: Callistus I
celebration.quote: I have the power to forgive all sins.
celebration.description: Pope who defended mercy for repentant sinners.

celebration.name: Teresa of Avila
celebration.quote: Let nothing disturb you, let nothing frighten you, all things pass away: God never changes.
celebration.description: Reformed Carmelites, Doctor of the Church, mystic who taught stages of prayer.

celebration.name: Hedwig
celebration.quote: All I have belongs to God and the poor.
celebration.description: Duchess of Silesia who used wealth to serve the poor.

celebration.name: Ignatius of Antioch
celebration.quote: I am the wheat of God, and am ground by the teeth of wild beasts.
celebration.description: Bishop and martyr who wrote seven letters while being taken to Rome for execution.

celebration.name: Luke
celebration.quote: I have decided to write an orderly account for you.
celebration.description: Evangelist, physician, companion of Paul, patron of artists and doctors.

celebration.name: John de Brebeuf and Isaac Jogues
celebration.quote: I came here to live for God alone.
celebration.description: North American martyrs, Jesuits killed evangelizing Native Americans.

celebration.name: Paul of the Cross
celebration.quote: Every pain of the crucified Jesus speaks of love.
celebration.description: Founded the Passionists to preach Christ's Passion.

celebration.name: John Paul II
celebration.quote: Do not be afraid. Open wide the doors for Christ.
celebration.description: Pope who helped end communism, promoted Divine Mercy and Theology of the Body.

celebration.name: John of Capistrano
celebration.quote: Those who hope in the Lord will never be defeated.
celebration.description: Franciscan preacher who rallied Christians against Turkish invasion.

celebration.name: Anthony Mary Claret
celebration.quote: My glory is the Cross of Christ.
celebration.description: Archbishop of Cuba, founded Claretians.

celebration.name: Simon and Jude
celebration.quote: Build yourselves up in your most holy faith.
celebration.description: Two of the twelve apostles, traditionally martyred together.

celebration.name: All Saints
celebration.quote: Blessed are they who are called to the wedding feast of the Lamb.
celebration.description: Celebrates all the saints in heaven, known and unknown.

celebration.name: All Souls
celebration.quote: Eternal rest grant unto them, O Lord.
celebration.description: Day of prayer for all the faithful departed.

celebration.name: Martin de Porres
celebration.quote: Compassion, my dear brother, is preferable to cleanliness.
celebration.description: Dominican brother, patron of social justice and racial harmony.

celebration.name: Charles Borromeo
celebration.quote: We were born to work together like feet, hands, and eyes.
celebration.description: Cardinal Archbishop of Milan who reformed the Church after Trent.

celebration.name: Dedication of the Lateran Basilica
celebration.quote: This is the house of God and the gate of heaven.
celebration.description: Mother church of all churches, the Pope's cathedral.

celebration.name: Leo the Great
celebration.quote: Christian, recognize your dignity.
celebration.description: Pope and Doctor who defended doctrine and saved Rome from Attila.

celebration.name: Martin of Tours
celebration.quote: I am Christ's soldier; I am not allowed to fight.
celebration.description: Soldier who shared his cloak with a beggar, became bishop.

celebration.name: Josaphat
celebration.quote: If I could give my life to unite the Churches, I would gladly do so.
celebration.description: Bishop and martyr who worked for unity between East and West.

celebration.name: Frances Xavier Cabrini
celebration.quote: We must pray without tiring, for the prayer of the just man can do much.
celebration.description: First American citizen to be canonized, patron of immigrants.

celebration.name: Albert the Great
celebration.quote: Wonder is the beginning of wisdom.
celebration.description: Dominican bishop, Doctor of the Church, teacher of Aquinas, scientist.

celebration.name: Margaret of Scotland
celebration.quote: Christ poor and crucified should be the model for all Christians.
celebration.description: Queen who reformed the Church in Scotland.

celebration.name: Elizabeth of Hungary
celebration.quote: How can I wear a crown of gold when the Lord Jesus wears a crown of thorns?
celebration.description: Princess who gave away her wealth to serve the poor.

celebration.name: Dedication of Basilicas of Peter and Paul
celebration.quote: You are fellow citizens with the saints and members of the household of God.
celebration.description: Commemorates the dedication of the two apostles' basilicas in Rome.

celebration.name: Presentation of Mary
celebration.quote: Here I am, the servant of the Lord.
celebration.description: Commemorates Mary's presentation in the Temple as a child.

celebration.name: Cecilia
celebration.quote: Sing to the Lord a new song.
celebration.description: Virgin martyr, patron of musicians.

celebration.name: Clement I
celebration.quote: Let us be humble minded and put aside all arrogance, pride and foolishness.
celebration.description: Fourth Pope, disciple of Peter, martyred by drowning.

celebration.name: Andrew Dung-Lac and Companions
celebration.quote: I die for God, and it is a joyful thing.
celebration.description: Vietnamese martyrs spanning three centuries.

celebration.name: Catherine of Alexandria
celebration.quote: I am the bride of Christ.
celebration.description: Virgin martyr, one of the Fourteen Holy Helpers.

celebration.name: Andrew
celebration.quote: Come and see!
celebration.description: First-called apostle, brother of Peter, patron of Scotland.

celebration.name: Francis Xavier
celebration.quote: Give me the children until they are seven and anyone may have them afterwards.
celebration.description: Jesuit missionary to Asia, patron of foreign missions.

celebration.name: John Damascene
celebration.quote: An image is a song of triumph, a revelation, an enduring monument to the victory of the saints.
celebration.description: Doctor of the Church who defended sacred images.

celebration.name: Nicholas
celebration.quote: The good must associate with the good.
celebration.description: Bishop of Myra known for generosity, basis for Santa Claus.

celebration.name: Ambrose
celebration.quote: When in Rome, do as the Romans do.
celebration.description: Bishop of Milan, Doctor of the Church, converted Augustine.

celebration.name: Immaculate Conception
celebration.quote: I am the Immaculate Conception.
celebration.description: Celebrates Mary's conception without original sin.

celebration.name: Juan Diego
celebration.quote: Am I not here, who am your Mother?
celebration.description: Visionary of Our Lady of Guadalupe.

celebration.name: Damasus I
celebration.quote: The Word of God must be given to all people.
celebration.description: Pope who commissioned Jerome to translate the Bible.

celebration.name: Our Lady of Guadalupe
celebration.quote: Do not be afraid. Am I not your Mother?
celebration.description: Patroness of the Americas, appeared to Juan Diego.

celebration.name: Lucy
celebration.quote: The light of Christ shines in me.
celebration.description: Virgin martyr, patron of the blind, name means light.

celebration.name: John of the Cross
celebration.quote: In the evening of life, we will be judged on love alone.
celebration.description: Carmelite mystic, Doctor of the Church, wrote Dark Night of the Soul.

celebration.name: Peter Canisius
celebration.quote: If you want to help souls, you must teach the catechism.
celebration.description: Jesuit who helped preserve Catholicism in Germany.

celebration.name: John of Kanty
celebration.quote: Fight all error, but do it with good humor, patience, kindness, and love.
celebration.description: Polish priest and professor known for charity.

celebration.name: Nativity of the Lord
celebration.quote: For unto you is born this day a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.
celebration.description: Christmas, the birth of Jesus Christ our Savior.

celebration.name: Stephen
celebration.quote: Lord, do not hold this sin against them.
celebration.description: First Christian martyr, one of the seven deacons.

celebration.name: John
celebration.quote: Little children, love one another.
celebration.description: Beloved disciple, apostle and evangelist.

celebration.name: Holy Innocents
celebration.quote: A voice was heard in Ramah, weeping and great mourning.
celebration.description: Children killed by Herod in his attempt to kill the infant Jesus.

celebration.name: Thomas Becket
celebration.quote: I am ready to die for my Lord.
celebration.description: Archbishop of Canterbury martyred in his cathedral.

celebration.name: Sylvester I
celebration.quote: Let us rejoice in the Lord, for He has given us peace.
celebration.description: Pope during Constantine's reign when Christianity became legal.

celebration.name: Mary, Mother of God
celebration.quote: Mary, the Mother of God, is our Mother also.
celebration.description: On the octave day of Christmas, the Church celebrates the Solemnity of Mary, the Holy Mother of God, honoring her role as the Mother of the Savior and the start of the civil New Year with a focus on peace.

celebration.name: Saints Basil the Great and Gregory Nazianzen, Bishops and Doctors
celebration.quote: When someone steals another's clothes, we call them a thief. Should we not give the same name to one who could clothe the naked and does not?
celebration.description: A pivotal figure of the early Church, instrumental in combating Arianism and organizing famine relief. His principles significantly influenced Eastern monasticism.

celebration.name: The Most Holy Name of Jesus
Epiphany
celebration.quote: They prostrated themselves and did him homage.
celebration.description: The Epiphany celebrates the revelation of the Savior to all nations, represented by the Magi who followed the star to worship the infant Jesus.

celebration.name: Saint Raymond of Penyafort, Priest
celebration.quote: Look then on Jesus, the Author and Preserver of faith: in complete sinlessness He suffered.
celebration.description: A Dominican friar and brilliant canon lawyer who compiled the 'Decretals' and served as Master General.

celebration.name: Baptism of the Lord
celebration.quote: You are my beloved Son; with you I am well pleased.
celebration.description: The Baptism of the Lord marks the beginning of Jesus' public ministry and reveals His identity as the Beloved Son of the Father.

celebration.name: Monday of the 1st week of Ordinary Time
Saint Hilary of Poitiers, Bishop and Doctor
celebration.quote: The chief service I owe you, O God, is that every thought and word of mine should speak of you.
celebration.description: Bishop of Poitiers and Doctor of the Church, known as the 'Athanasius of the West' for his defense of the Trinity against Arianism.

celebration.name: Wednesday of the 1st week of Ordinary Time
Thursday of the 1st week of Ordinary Time
Friday of the 1st week of Ordinary Time
Saint Anthony of Egypt, Abbot
celebration.quote: The devil is afraid of us when we pray and make sacrifices. He is also afraid when we are humble and good.
celebration.description: A pioneer of Christian monasticism in the desert, known for his asceticism and his victories over spiritual temptations.

celebration.name: Monday of the 2nd week of Ordinary Time
Saints Fabian, Pope, and Sebastian, Martyrs
celebration.quote: We must remember that we are not to seek our own will, but the will of God.
celebration.description: A Roman layman miraculously chosen as Pope, he served for 14 years and was martyred during the persecution of Decius.

celebration.name: Saint Agnes, Virgin and Martyr
celebration.quote: Christ made my soul beautiful with the jewels of grace and virtue. I belong to Him whom the angels serve.
celebration.description: A young virgin martyr in Rome who dedicated herself to Christ at a young age, refusing to renounce her faith despite persecution.

celebration.name: Saint Vincent, Deacon and Martyr
celebration.quote: The most powerful weapon to conquer the devil is humility.
celebration.description: A deacon and martyr of Spain, known for his unwavering faith and his eloquent testimony before his executioners.

celebration.name: Friday of the 2nd week of Ordinary Time
Saint Francis de Sales, Bishop and Doctor
celebration.quote: Nothing is so strong as gentleness, nothing so gentle as real strength.
celebration.description: Bishop of Geneva and a Doctor of the Church, he is the patron of writers and journalists, known for his gentleness.

celebration.name: Saints Timothy and Titus, Bishops
celebration.quote: Let no one despise your youth, but be an example to the believers in word, in conduct, in love, in spirit, in faith, in purity.
celebration.description: A close companion of St. Paul and the first Bishop of Ephesus, known for his youth and dedicated service to the early Church.

celebration.name: Saint Angela Merici, Virgin
celebration.quote: Do something. Get moving. Be confident. Risk new things. Stick with it. Get on your knees. Then be ready for big surprises.
celebration.description: Foundress of the Ursulines, she dedicated her life to the education of young girls and promoting the role of women in the Church.

celebration.name: Saint Thomas Aquinas, Priest and Doctor
celebration.quote: To one who has faith, no explanation is necessary.
celebration.description: The 'Angelic Doctor,' his monumental 'Summa Theologiae' remains a foundational work of theology.

celebration.name: Thursday of the 3rd week of Ordinary Time
Friday of the 3rd week of Ordinary Time
Saint John Bosco, Priest
celebration.quote: Run, jump, shout, but do not sin.
celebration.description: The 'Father and Teacher of Youth,' he founded the Salesians to care for and educate young people, especially the poor and abandoned.

celebration.name: Saint Blase, Bishop Martyr and Saint Ansgar, Bishop
celebration.quote: O Lord, deliver us from all evil of body and soul.
celebration.description: Bishop and martyr known for his miraculous healing of a boy with a bone in his throat; he is the patron of those with throat ailments.

celebration.name: Wednesday of the 4th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Agatha, Virgin and Martyr
celebration.quote: Jesus Christ, Lord of all, you see my heart, you know my desires; possess all that I am, you alone.
celebration.description: A young virgin martyr in Sicily who bravely endured torture and death rather than sacrifice her faith or her purity.

celebration.name: Saint Paul Miki and Companions, Martyrs
celebration.quote: My religion teaches me to pardon my enemies and all who have offended me.
celebration.description: A Japanese Jesuit who was crucified in Nagasaki, preaching the Gospel and forgiving his executioners from the cross.

celebration.name: Saturday of the 4th week of Ordinary Time
Monday of the 5th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Scholastica, Virgin
celebration.quote: I asked you and you would not listen, so I asked my God and He did listen.
celebration.description: The twin sister of St. Benedict, she founded a community of nuns and is remembered for her spiritual conversations.

celebration.name: Thursday of the 5th week of Ordinary Time
Friday of the 5th week of Ordinary Time
Saints Cyril, Monk and Methodius, Bishop
celebration.quote: I am ready with joy to die for the Christian faith.
celebration.description: A Greek missionary who, with his brother Methodius, translated the liturgy into Slavonic and created the Cyrillic alphabet.

celebration.name: Monday of the 6th week of Ordinary Time
Seven Holy Founders of the Servite Order
Saint Peter Damian, Bishop and Doctor of the Church
celebration.quote: He does not love Christ who does not love the cross of Christ.
celebration.description: A monk, cardinal, and reformer, he was a powerful voice for Church renewal and advocate for clerical discipline.

celebration.name: Saint Polycarp, Bishop and Martyr
celebration.quote: We worship the Creator of the universe, and we affirm that Jesus Christ is His Son.
celebration.description: A philosopher who converted to Christianity and became its first great apologist, eventually martyred for refusing to sacrifice to idols.

celebration.name: Tuesday of the 1st week of Lent
Wednesday of the 1st week of Lent
Thursday of the 1st week of Lent
Friday of the 1st week of Lent
Saturday of the 1st week of Lent
Monday of the 2nd week of Lent
Tuesday of the 2nd week of Lent
Saint Casimir
celebration.quote: I prefer to be found pure before God rather than stained with the riches of the world.
celebration.description: A Polish prince who chose a life of humility, chastity, and devotion over royal luxury, dying at the age of 25.

celebration.name: Thursday of the 2nd week of Lent
Friday of the 2nd week of Lent
Saints Perpetua and Felicity, Martyrs
celebration.quote: I cannot be called by any other name than what I am—a Christian.
celebration.description: A young noblewoman and nursing mother martyred in Carthage; her prison diary is one of the earliest accounts of Christian martyrdom.

celebration.name: Saint Frances of Rome, Religious
celebration.quote: A married woman must leave her devotions at the altar to find God in her household affairs.
celebration.description: A noblewoman who balanced family duties with devotion and service, eventually founding a community of oblates.

celebration.name: Tuesday of the 3rd week of Lent
Wednesday of the 3rd week of Lent
Thursday of the 3rd week of Lent
Friday of the 3rd week of Lent
Saturday of the 3rd week of Lent
Monday of the 4th week of Lent
Saint Patrick, Bishop
celebration.quote: Christ beside me, Christ before me, Christ behind me, Christ within me.
celebration.description: The patron saint of Ireland who returned to evangelize the island and establish the Church firmly among the Irish people.

celebration.name: Saint Cyril of Jerusalem, Bishop and Doctor
celebration.quote: The cross has ransomed the world; it has driven away the shadows and restored the light.
celebration.description: Bishop of Jerusalem and Doctor of the Church, known for his 'Catechetical Lectures' explaining the faith to new Christians.

celebration.name: Joseph, Husband of Mary
celebration.quote: Work was the daily expression of love in the life of the Family of Nazareth.
celebration.description: The foster father of Jesus as a model of labor and the dignity of work, honoring his role as a carpenter in Nazareth.

celebration.name: Friday of the 4th week of Lent
Saturday of the 4th week of Lent
Saint Turibius of Mogrovejo, Bishop
celebration.quote: Time is not our own, and we must give a strict account of it.
celebration.description: As Archbishop of Lima, he tirelessly evangelized South America and served the indigenous peoples.

celebration.name: Tuesday of the 5th week of Lent
Thursday of the 5th week of Lent
Friday of the 5th week of Lent
Saturday of the 5th week of Lent
Saint Martin I, Pope and Martyr
celebration.quote: I fear not exile or death; I fear only failing the truth.
celebration.description: The last martyr Pope, he was exiled and suffered for his defense of Christ's two wills against the Monothelite heresy.

celebration.name: Tuesday of the 2nd week of Easter
Wednesday of the 2nd week of Easter
Thursday of the 2nd week of Easter
Friday of the 2nd week of Easter
Saturday of the 2nd week of Easter
Monday of the 3rd week of Easter
Saint Anselm of Canterbury, Bishop and Doctor of the Church
celebration.quote: I believe in order to understand.
celebration.description: The 'Father of Scholasticism,' he was a philosopher and Archbishop of Canterbury who sought to understand faith through reason.

celebration.name: Wednesday of the 3rd week of Easter
Saint George, Martyr/Saint Adalbert, Bishop and Martyr
celebration.quote: We worship the Creator of the universe, and we affirm that Jesus Christ is His Son.
celebration.description: A philosopher who converted to Christianity and became its first great apologist, eventually martyred for refusing to sacrifice to idols.

celebration.name: Saint Fidelis of Sigmaringen, Priest and Martyr
celebration.quote: We worship the Creator of the universe, and we affirm that Jesus Christ is His Son.
celebration.description: A philosopher who converted to Christianity and became its first great apologist, eventually martyred for refusing to sacrifice to idols.

celebration.name: Saint Mark the Evangelist
celebration.quote: Go into all the world and preach the gospel to every creature.
celebration.description: The author of the second Gospel and companion of St. Peter, he brought the Gospel to Egypt and founded the Church in Alexandria.

celebration.name: Monday of the 4th week of Easter
Saint Peter Chanel, Priest and Martyr/Saint Louis Grignon de Montfort, Priest
celebration.quote: We worship the Creator of the universe, and we affirm that Jesus Christ is His Son.
celebration.description: A philosopher who converted to Christianity and became its first great apologist, eventually martyred for refusing to sacrifice to idols.

celebration.name: Saint Catherine of Siena, Virgin and Doctor of The Church, Patron of Europe
celebration.quote: Be who God meant you to be and you will set the world on fire.
celebration.description: A mystic, advisor to Popes, and Doctor of the Church who worked for Church reform and unity through her prayers and writings.

celebration.name: Saint Pius V, Pope
celebration.quote: God does not ask of you mere ordinary virtue. He demands downright perfection!
celebration.description: A Dominican Pope who implemented the reforms of the Council of Trent and standardized the Roman Missal.

celebration.name: Saint Joseph the Worker
celebration.quote: Work was the daily expression of love in the life of the Family of Nazareth.
celebration.description: The foster father of Jesus as a model of labor and the dignity of work, honoring his role as a carpenter in Nazareth.

celebration.name: Saint Athanasius, Bishop and Doctor
celebration.quote: The Son of God became man so that we might become God.
celebration.description: The 'Hammer of Orthodoxy' who defended the divinity of Christ against Arianism during five periods of exile.

celebration.name: Monday of the 5th week of Easter
Tuesday of the 5th week of Easter
Wednesday of the 5th week of Easter
Thursday of the 5th week of Easter
Friday of the 5th week of Easter
Saturday of the 5th week of Easter
Monday of the 6th week of Easter
Saints Nereus and Achilleus, Martyrs/Saint Pancras, Martyr
celebration.quote: We worship the Creator of the universe, and we affirm that Jesus Christ is His Son.
celebration.description: A philosopher who converted to Christianity and became its first great apologist, eventually martyred for refusing to sacrifice to idols.

celebration.name: Friday of the 6th week of Easter
Saturday of the 6th week of Easter
Saint John I, Pope and Martyr
celebration.quote: The peace of Christ in the kingdom of Christ.
celebration.description: A Pope and martyr who was imprisoned by the Arian King Theodoric after a diplomatic mission to Constantinople.

celebration.name: Tuesday of the 7th week of Easter
Saint Bernardine of Siena, Priest
celebration.quote: The name of Jesus is a glory for the preacher, because of the shining splendor of that name.
celebration.description: A Franciscan preacher who promoted devotion to the Holy Name of Jesus and reformed his order with great zeal.

celebration.name: Saint Christopher Magallanes and Companions, Martyrs
celebration.quote: I am innocent and I die innocent. I forgive with all my heart those responsible for my death.
celebration.description: A Mexican priest martyred during the Cristero War for his faith, forgiving his executioners with his last breath.

celebration.name: Saint Rita of Cascia
celebration.quote: I am not afraid to die. It is to close the eyes to the world and open them to God.
celebration.description: Called the 'Patroness of Impossible Causes,' she was a wife, mother, widow, and nun known for her patience.

celebration.name: Saturday of the 7th week of Easter
Mary, Mother of The Church
celebration.quote: Mary, the Mother of God, is our Mother also.
celebration.description: On the octave day of Christmas, the Church celebrates the Solemnity of Mary, the Holy Mother of God, honoring her role as the Mother of the Savior and the start of the civil New Year with a focus on peace.

celebration.name: Saint Philip Neri, Priest
celebration.quote: A joyful heart is more easily made perfect than a downcast one.
celebration.description: The 'Apostle of Rome' and founder of the Oratory, he was known for his humor, joy, and leading people to deep spiritual lives.

celebration.name: Saint Augustine of Canterbury, Bishop
celebration.quote: He who sings prays twice.
celebration.description: The 'Apostle of the English,' he was sent by Pope Gregory the Great to convert the Anglo-Saxons and became the first Archbishop of Canterbury.

celebration.name: Thursday of the 8th week of Ordinary Time
Friday of the 8th week of Ordinary Time
Saturday of the 8th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Justin Martyr
celebration.quote: We worship the Creator of the universe, and we affirm that Jesus Christ is His Son.
celebration.description: A philosopher who converted to Christianity and became its first great apologist, eventually martyred for refusing to sacrifice to idols.

celebration.name: Saints Marcellinus and Peter, Martyrs
celebration.quote: If you have too much to do, with God's help you will find time to do it all.
celebration.description: A Jesuit known as the 'Second Apostle of Germany,' he played a vital role in the Counter-Reformation.

celebration.name: Saints Charles Lwanga and Companions, Martyrs
celebration.quote: A Christian who is convinced that his life is in the hands of God has no reason to fear.
celebration.description: A young catechist in Uganda who was martyred along with his companions for their refusal to compromise their faith and purity.

celebration.name: Thursday of the 9th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Boniface, Bishop and Martyr
celebration.quote: We worship the Creator of the universe, and we affirm that Jesus Christ is His Son.
celebration.description: A philosopher who converted to Christianity and became its first great apologist, eventually martyred for refusing to sacrifice to idols.

celebration.name: Saint Norbert, Bishop
celebration.quote: A talkative, over-curious person is like an oven which is open and exposed on all sides.
celebration.description: After a dramatic conversion, he founded the Premonstratensian Order and became Archbishop of Magdeburg.

celebration.name: Monday of the 10th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Ephrem, Deacon and Doctor
celebration.quote: Virtue is the beauty of the soul.
celebration.description: The 'Harp of the Holy Spirit,' he was a Syrian deacon and poet whose hymns and theology defended the faith against early heresies.

celebration.name: Wednesday of the 10th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Barnabas the Apostle
celebration.quote: Pray as though everything depended on God. Work as though everything depended on you.
celebration.description: The 'Son of Encouragement' who vouched for St. Paul and accompanied him on his first missionary journey to the Gentiles.

celebration.name: Monday of the 11th week of Ordinary Time
Tuesday of the 11th week of Ordinary Time
Wednesday of the 11th week of Ordinary Time
Thursday of the 11th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Romuald, Abbot
celebration.quote: Sit in your cell as in paradise. Put the whole world behind you and forget it.
celebration.description: Founder of the Camaldolese order, he promoted a strict hermit-like life based on the Rule of St. Benedict.

celebration.name: Saturday of the 11th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Paulinus of Nola, Bishop/Saints John Fisher, Bishop and Thomas More, Martyrs
celebration.quote: O Lord, let me not be troubled for gold and silver, for where all my treasure is Thou knowest.
celebration.description: A wealthy Roman nobleman who gave up everything to become a priest and then Bishop of Nola, known for his poetry.

celebration.name: Tuesday of the 12th week of Ordinary Time
Birth of John the Baptist
celebration.quote: He must increase, but I must decrease.
celebration.description: The precursor of Jesus who prepared the way of the Lord through his preaching of repentance and baptism in the Jordan.

celebration.name: Thursday of the 12th week of Ordinary Time
Friday of the 12th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Cyril of Alexandria, Bishop and Doctor
celebration.quote: Mary, mother of God, who held the Uncontainable in your womb.
celebration.description: Bishop and Doctor of the Church who defended the title of Mary as 'Theotokos' (Mother of God) at the Council of Ephesus.

celebration.name: Saints Peter and Paul, Apostles
celebration.quote: Be not afraid! Open wide the doors for Christ!
celebration.description: The first Polish Pope, he traveled extensively, calling all to 'Be not afraid' and opening the Church to the modern world.

celebration.name: First Martyrs of the Church of Rome
Wednesday of the 13th week of Ordinary Time
Thursday of the 13th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Thomas the Apostle
celebration.quote: My Lord and my God!
celebration.description: Initially doubting the Resurrection, his encounter with the Risen Lord led to his famous profession of faith.

celebration.name: Saint Elizabeth of Portugal
celebration.quote: The only reason God raised me to the throne was that I should have means to assist the needy.
celebration.description: A princess of Aragon and Queen of Portugal, she was a peacemaker between kings and a friend to the poor.

celebration.name: Saint Maria Goretti, Virgin and Martyr
celebration.quote: We worship the Creator of the universe, and we affirm that Jesus Christ is His Son.
celebration.description: A philosopher who converted to Christianity and became its first great apologist, eventually martyred for refusing to sacrifice to idols.

celebration.name: Tuesday of the 14th week of Ordinary Time
Wednesday of the 14th week of Ordinary Time
Saints Augustine Zhao Rong, Priest, and Companions, Martyrs
celebration.quote: Mary, the Mother of God, is our Mother also.
celebration.description: On the octave day of Christmas, the Church celebrates the Solemnity of Mary, the Holy Mother of God, honoring her role as the Mother of the Savior and the start of the civil New Year with a focus on peace.

celebration.name: Friday of the 14th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Benedict of Nursia, Abbot, Patron of Europe
celebration.quote: Prayer and work. (Ora et labora)
celebration.description: The 'Father of Western Monasticism,' he founded the monastery at Monte Cassino and wrote the famous Rule of St. Benedict.

celebration.name: Saint Henry, Bishop and Martyr
celebration.quote: We worship the Creator of the universe, and we affirm that Jesus Christ is His Son.
celebration.description: A philosopher who converted to Christianity and became its first great apologist, eventually martyred for refusing to sacrifice to idols.

celebration.name: Saint Camillus de Lellis, Priest
celebration.quote: Pray, hope, and don't worry. Worry is useless.
celebration.description: A Capuchin friar known as Padre Pio, he bore the stigmata for fifty years and was a renowned confessor.

celebration.name: Saint Bonaventure, Bishop and Doctor
celebration.quote: The best way to know God is to love Him.
celebration.description: The 'Seraphic Doctor,' he was a Franciscan theologian, philosopher, and Cardinal who served as Minister General of his order.

celebration.name: Friday of the 15th week of Ordinary Time
Saturday of the 15th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Apollinaris
celebration.quote: I believe in Jesus Christ, the Son of the living God.
celebration.description: Traditionally the first Bishop of Ravenna and a disciple of St. Peter, he was martyred for his steadfast witness to Christ.

celebration.name: Saint Lawrence of Brindisi, Priest and Doctor
celebration.quote: Pray, hope, and don't worry. Worry is useless.
celebration.description: A Capuchin friar known as Padre Pio, he bore the stigmata for fifty years and was a renowned confessor.

celebration.name: Saint Mary Magdalene
celebration.quote: I have seen the Lord!
celebration.description: The 'Apostle to the Apostles,' she was the first to witness the Resurrection and was sent by Jesus to tell the others.

celebration.name: Saint Bridget of Sweden, Religious, Patron of Europe
celebration.quote: How could I bear a crown of gold when the Lord bears a crown of thorns?
celebration.description: Born in 1207, St. Elizabeth was a princess who devoted her life and wealth to the poor, joining the Third Order of St. Francis.

celebration.name: Saint Charbel Makhlouf, Priest and Hermit
celebration.quote: Pray, hope, and don't worry. Worry is useless.
celebration.description: A Capuchin friar known as Padre Pio, he bore the stigmata for fifty years and was a renowned confessor.

celebration.name: Saint James, Apostle
celebration.quote: My life is for the service of the Gospel.
celebration.description: James the Greater, the first apostle to be martyred, he was part of Jesus' inner circle and is the patron of Spain.

celebration.name: Monday of the 17th week of Ordinary Time
Tuesday of the 17th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Martha
celebration.quote: Yes, Lord. I have come to believe that you are the Christ, the Son of God.
celebration.description: A close friend of Jesus and sister of Mary and Lazarus, known for her hospitality and her firm profession of faith in the Resurrection.

celebration.name: Saint Peter Chrysologus, Bishop and Doctor
celebration.quote: Fasting is the soul of prayer, almsgiving is the lifeblood of fasting.
celebration.description: Known as 'golden-worded' for his eloquent preaching, he was the Bishop of Ravenna and a strong defender of the Incarnation.

celebration.name: Saint Ignatius of Loyola, Priest
celebration.quote: Lord, teach me to be generous. Teach me to serve you as you deserve.
celebration.description: A soldier turned spiritual master, he founded the Society of Jesus (Jesuits) and wrote the 'Spiritual Exercises'.

celebration.name: Saint Alphonsus Maria de Liguori, Bishop and Doctor of the Church
celebration.quote: He who trusts himself is lost. He who trusts God can do all things.
celebration.description: Founder of the Redemptorists and a Doctor of the Church, he is the patron of confessors and moral theologians.

celebration.name: Monday of the 18th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Jean Vianney (the Cure of Ars), Priest
celebration.quote: My God, if my tongue cannot say in every moment that I love You, I want my heart to repeat it at every breath.
celebration.description: The Curé of Ars and patron of parish priests, known for his incredible dedication to the confessional and his simple, profound holiness.

celebration.name: Dedication of the Basilica of Saint Mary Major
celebration.quote: Holy Mary, Mother of God, pray for us.
celebration.description: Commemorates the dedication of the largest and oldest church in Rome dedicated to the Virgin Mary, a center of Marian devotion.

celebration.name: Saint Sixtus II, Pope, and Companions, Martyrs/Saint Cajetan, Priest
celebration.quote: Mary, the Mother of God, is our Mother also.
celebration.description: On the octave day of Christmas, the Church celebrates the Solemnity of Mary, the Holy Mother of God, honoring her role as the Mother of the Savior and the start of the civil New Year with a focus on peace.

celebration.name: Saint Dominic, Priest
celebration.quote: Arm yourself with prayer rather than a sword; wear humility rather than fine clothes.
celebration.description: Founder of the Dominicans who promoted study, prayer, and preaching to defend the faith against the Albigensian heresy.

celebration.name: Saint Lawrence of Rome, Deacon and Martyr
celebration.quote: The treasures of the Church are the poor.
celebration.description: A Roman deacon martyred on a gridiron, famously telling his executioners, 'I am well done on this side; turn me over!'.

celebration.name: Saint Clare, Virgin
celebration.quote: We become what we love and who we love shapes what we become.
celebration.description: A noblewoman who followed St. Francis and founded the Poor Clares, living a life of extreme poverty and contemplative prayer.

celebration.name: Saint Jane Frances de Chantal, Religious
celebration.quote: Faithfully do what God expects of you each moment, and leave the rest up to Him.
celebration.description: A widow and mother who, with St. Francis de Sales, founded the Order of the Visitation.

celebration.name: Saints Pontian, Pope and Hippolytus, Priest, Martyrs
celebration.quote: The Lord is our strength and our shield.
celebration.description: A Pope who was exiled to the mines of Sardinia and resigned his office to allow for a successor, dying as a martyr.

celebration.name: Saint Maximilian Mary Kolbe, Priest and Martyr
celebration.quote: No one in the world can change Truth. What we can do and should do is to seek truth and to serve it when we have found it.
celebration.description: A Franciscan who gave his life for a fellow prisoner at Auschwitz, a martyr of charity and devotee of the Immaculata.

celebration.name: The Assumption of the Blessed Virgin Mary
Monday of the 20th week of Ordinary Time
Tuesday of the 20th week of Ordinary Time
Saint John Eudes, Priest
celebration.quote: Our wish, our object, our chief preoccupation must be to form Jesus in ourselves.
celebration.description: The 'Apostle of Two Hearts,' he founded the Eudists and promoted devotion to the Sacred Heart of Jesus and the Immaculate Heart of Mary.

celebration.name: Saint Bernard of Clairvaux, Abbot and Doctor of the Church
celebration.quote: The measure of loving God is to love Him without measure.
celebration.description: A Cistercian abbot and Doctor of the Church whose preaching and writings significantly influenced medieval spirituality.

celebration.name: Saint Pius X, Pope
celebration.quote: Holy Communion is the shortest and safest way to Heaven.
celebration.description: Known for his motto 'To restore all things in Christ,' he promoted frequent Holy Communion and fought Modernism.

celebration.name: Saint Bartholomew the Apostle
celebration.quote: Rabbi, you are the Son of God; you are the King of Israel.
celebration.description: One of the Twelve Apostles, also known as Nathanael, who is believed to have preached in India and Armenia before being flayed alive.

celebration.name: Saint Louis/Saint Joseph of Calasanz, Priest
celebration.quote: I would rather see you dead at my feet than that you should ever commit a mortal sin.
celebration.description: King Louis IX of France, a model of Christian leadership and justice who was deeply devoted to the poor and to the Church.

celebration.name: Wednesday of the 21st week of Ordinary Time
Saint Monica
celebration.quote: Nothing is far from God.
celebration.description: The mother of St. Augustine whose persistent prayers and tears over many years led to the conversion of her son and husband.

celebration.name: Saint Augustine of Hippo, Bishop and Doctor of the Church
celebration.quote: Thou hast made us for thyself, O Lord, and our heart is restless until it finds its rest in thee.
celebration.description: One of the most influential theologians, his conversion and writings like 'Confessions' shaped Western Christianity.

celebration.name: The Beheading of Saint John the Baptist, Martyr
celebration.quote: He must increase, but I must decrease.
celebration.description: The precursor of Jesus who prepared the way of the Lord through his preaching of repentance and baptism in the Jordan.

celebration.name: Monday of the 22nd week of Ordinary Time
Tuesday of the 22nd week of Ordinary Time
Wednesday of the 22nd week of Ordinary Time
Saint Gregory the Great, Pope and Doctor of the Church
celebration.quote: Learn of the Heart of God in the Words of God.
celebration.description: A monk who became one of the greatest Popes, he reformed the liturgy and sent missionaries to convert England.

celebration.name: Friday of the 22nd week of Ordinary Time
Saturday of the 22nd week of Ordinary Time
Monday of the 23rd week of Ordinary Time
Birth of the Blessed Virgin Mary
Saint Peter Claver, Priest
celebration.quote: We must speak to them with our hands by giving, before we try to speak to them with our lips.
celebration.description: A Jesuit who spent 40 years in Colombia ministering to enslaved Africans arriving on slave ships.

celebration.name: Thursday of the 23rd week of Ordinary Time
Friday of the 23rd week of Ordinary Time
Holy Name of the Blessed Virgin Mary
The Exaltation of the Holy Cross
Saints Cornelius, Pope, and Cyprian, Bishop, Martyrs
celebration.quote: Let us relieve burdens and afflictions by mutual love.
celebration.description: A Pope who advocated for the reconciliation of those who had lapsed during persecution and was eventually martyred.

celebration.name: Saint Robert Bellarmine, Bishop and Doctor of the Church
celebration.quote: Whoever finds God, finds everything; whoever loses God, loses everything.
celebration.description: A Jesuit cardinal and scholar, he was a key figure in the Counter-Reformation, providing robust defenses of doctrine.

celebration.name: Friday of the 24th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Januarius, Bishop and Martyr
celebration.quote: My power is nothing but there is a God in heaven who can resist you.
celebration.description: Bishop and martyr whose blood is said to liquefy miraculously in Naples as a sign of his intercession and the presence of God.

celebration.name: Saint Matthew, Apostle and Evangelist
celebration.quote: Follow me.
celebration.description: A tax collector called by Jesus to be an Apostle and Evangelist, writing his Gospel to show that Jesus is the fulfillment of the law.

celebration.name: Tuesday of the 25th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Pio of Pietrelcina (Padre Pio), Priest
celebration.quote: Pray, hope, and don't worry. Worry is useless.
celebration.description: Padre Pio, a Capuchin friar who bore the stigmata and was a renowned confessor and spiritual guide to thousands.

celebration.name: Thursday of the 25th week of Ordinary Time
Friday of the 25th week of Ordinary Time
Saints Cosmas and Damian, Martyrs
celebration.quote: He does not love Christ who does not love the cross of Christ.
celebration.description: A monk, cardinal, and reformer, he was a powerful voice for Church renewal and advocate for clerical discipline.

celebration.name: Saint Wenceslaus, Martyr/Saints Lawrence Ruiz and Companions, Martyrs
celebration.quote: We worship the Creator of the universe, and we affirm that Jesus Christ is His Son.
celebration.description: A philosopher who converted to Christianity and became its first great apologist, eventually martyred for refusing to sacrifice to idols.

celebration.name: Saints Michael, Gabriel and Raphael, Archangels
celebration.quote: Bless the Lord, all you his angels, mighty in power, who do his bidding.
celebration.description: The feast of the three Archangels mentioned by name in Scripture, who serve as messengers and protectors of God's people.

celebration.name: Saint Jerome, Priest and Doctor
celebration.quote: Ignorance of the Scriptures is ignorance of Christ.
celebration.description: A Doctor of the Church best known for his translation of the Bible into Latin (the Vulgate) and his deep love for Scripture.

celebration.name: Saint Therese of the Child Jesus, Virgin and Doctor
celebration.quote: I will spend my heaven doing good on earth.
celebration.description: The 'Little Flower' who taught the 'Little Way' of performing small acts with great love, a Doctor of the Church.

celebration.name: Saturday of the 26th week of Ordinary Time
Monday of the 27th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Bruno, Priest
celebration.quote: Pray, hope, and don't worry. Worry is useless.
celebration.description: A Capuchin friar known as Padre Pio, he bore the stigmata for fifty years and was a renowned confessor.

celebration.name: Thursday of the 27th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Denis and Companions Martyrs/Saint John Leonardi, Priest
celebration.quote: To live is Christ, to die is gain.
celebration.description: The first Bishop of Paris and martyr, traditionally depicted carrying his own head after being decapitated for his faith.

celebration.name: Saturday of the 27th week of Ordinary Time
Monday of the 28th week of Ordinary Time
Tuesday of the 28th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Callistus I, Pope and Martyr
celebration.quote: If offences abound, then, let mercy also abound.
celebration.description: A slave who became Pope, he emphasized mercy and forgiveness for sinners and was martyred in Rome.

celebration.name: Saint Teresa of Jesus, Virgin and Doctor
celebration.quote: Let nothing disturb you, let nothing frighten you. God alone suffices.
celebration.description: A mystic and reformer of the Carmelites, she is a Doctor of the Church known for her writings on prayer.

celebration.name: Saint Hedwig, Religious/Saint Margaret Mary Alacoque, Virgin
celebration.quote: The greater one is by birth, the greater one must be in virtue.
celebration.description: Duchess of Silesia who lived a life of profound humility and charity, caring for the poor and the sick in her regional hospitals.

celebration.name: Saint Ignatius of Antioch, Bishop and Martyr
celebration.quote: Wherever the bishop appears, there let the people be.
celebration.description: A disciple of St. John, he was martyred in Rome, leaving behind letters emphasizing Church unity and the Eucharist.

celebration.name: Saints Jean de Brebeuf and Isaac Jogues, Priests and Companions, Martyrs/Saint Paul of the Cross, Priest
celebration.quote: The greater our cross, the greater is our gain.
celebration.description: The founder of the Passionists, he was a mystic who dedicated his life to promoting devotion to the Passion of Christ.

celebration.name: Tuesday of the 29th week of Ordinary Time
Wednesday of the 29th week of Ordinary Time
Pope Saint John Paul II
celebration.quote: Be not afraid! Open wide the doors for Christ!
celebration.description: The first Polish Pope, he traveled extensively, calling all to 'Be not afraid' and opening the Church to the modern world.

celebration.name: Saint John of Capistrano, Priest
celebration.quote: Those who are called to the table of the Lord must glow with the brightness that comes from the good example.
celebration.description: A Franciscan preacher and soldier of Christ who led a successful defense against the Ottoman Turks through his preaching.

celebration.name: Saint Anthony Mary Claret, Bishop
celebration.quote: We become what we love and who we love shapes what we become.
celebration.description: A noblewoman who followed St. Francis and founded the Poor Clares, living a life of extreme poverty and contemplative prayer.

celebration.name: Monday of the 30th week of Ordinary Time
Tuesday of the 30th week of Ordinary Time
Saints Simon and Jude, Apostles
celebration.quote: To serve Christ is to reign.
celebration.description: One of the Twelve Apostles, known as 'the Zealot,' who preached the Gospel with great fervor before being martyred.

celebration.name: Thursday of the 30th week of Ordinary Time
Friday of the 30th week of Ordinary Time
Saturday of the 30th week of Ordinary Time
All Saints
celebration.quote: Rejoice and be glad, for your reward will be great in heaven.
celebration.description: The Solemnity of All Saints honors all the saints in heaven, known and unknown, who intercede for the Church on earth.

celebration.name: Saint Martin de Porres, Religious
celebration.quote: Everything, even sweeping... could be a prayer, if it were offered to God.
celebration.description: A humble Dominican lay brother known for his miracles, his care for the marginalized, and his love for all of God's creatures.

celebration.name: Saint Charles Borromeo, Bishop
celebration.quote: Be sure that you first preach by the way you live.
celebration.description: Archbishop of Milan and key figure in the Catholic Reformation, known for his pastoral zeal and implementation of Church reforms.

celebration.name: Thursday of the 31st week of Ordinary Time
Friday of the 31st week of Ordinary Time
Saturday of the 31st week of Ordinary Time
