Saint Leo the Great, Pope and Doctor
celebration.quote: Christian, recognize your dignity and, now that you share in God’s own nature, do not return to your former base condition by sinning.
celebration.description: A powerful Pope and Doctor of the Church who defended the city of Rome from Attila the Hun and clarified the two natures of Christ.

celebration.name: Saint Martin of Tours, Bishop
celebration.quote: Lord, if your people still need me, I do not draw back from the labor. Your will be done.
celebration.description: A Roman soldier who shared his cloak with a beggar, discovered to be Christ, and later became the beloved Bishop of Tours.

celebration.name: Saint Josaphat, Bishop and Martyr
celebration.quote: I am ready to die for the holy union.
celebration.description: An Eastern Catholic bishop and martyr who worked tirelessly for the unity of the Church, eventually giving his life for this cause.

celebration.name: Friday of the 32nd week of Ordinary Time
Saturday of the 32nd week of Ordinary Time
Saint Margaret of Scotland/Saint Gertrude the Great, Virgin
celebration.quote: My children, love and fear God; for they who fear God, have not to fear death.
celebration.description: Queen of Scotland who transformed the royal court with her piety, charity, and wisdom, becoming a model of Christian motherhood.

celebration.name: Saint Elizabeth of Hungary
celebration.quote: How could I bear a crown of gold when the Lord bears a crown of thorns?
celebration.description: Born in 1207, St. Elizabeth was a princess who devoted her life and wealth to the poor, joining the Third Order of St. Francis.

celebration.name: Dedication of the basilicas of Saints Peter and Paul, Apostles
celebration.quote: Be not afraid! Open wide the doors for Christ!
celebration.description: The first Polish Pope, he traveled extensively, calling all to 'Be not afraid' and opening the Church to the modern world.

celebration.name: Thursday of the 33rd week of Ordinary Time
Friday of the 33rd week of Ordinary Time
Presentation of The Blessed Virgin Mary
Saint Clement I, Pope and Martyr/Saint Columban, Religious
celebration.quote: Let us fix our eyes on the blood of Christ and understand how precious it is to His Father.
celebration.description: The fourth Pope, whose letter to the Corinthians remains one of the earliest and most important documents of the early Church.

celebration.name: Saint Andrew Dung-Lac and Companions, Martyrs
celebration.quote: The blood of martyrs is the seed of Christians.
celebration.description: One of the 117 Vietnamese martyrs who gave their lives for the faith during centuries of fierce persecution in Vietnam.

celebration.name: Saint Catherine of Alexandria, Virgin and Martyr
celebration.quote: If you are ruled by the mind you are king, if by the body you are a slave.
celebration.description: A brilliant young philosopher who converted many to Christ before being martyred on a wheel for her faith.

celebration.name: Thursday of the 34th week of Ordinary Time
Friday of the 34th week of Ordinary Time
Saturday of the 34th week of Ordinary Time
Saint Andrew the Apostle
celebration.quote: We have found the Messiah.
celebration.description: The first apostle called by Jesus, who immediately brought his brother Peter to the Lord and later preached in Greece.

celebration.name: Tuesday of the 1st week of Advent
Wednesday of the 1st week of Advent
Saint Francis Xavier, Priest
celebration.quote: Give me the children until they are seven, and anyone may have them afterward.
celebration.description: A founding member of the Jesuits and patron of missionaries, he spent his life evangelizing in Asia.

celebration.name: Saint John Damascene, Priest and Doctor
celebration.quote: The icon is a mirror, a window to the divine.
celebration.description: A Syrian monk and Doctor of the Church who defended the use of holy icons during the iconoclast controversy.

celebration.name: Saturday of the 1st week of Advent
Saint Ambrose, Bishop and Doctor
celebration.quote: Where the Church is, there is no death, but life eternal.
celebration.description: Bishop of Milan and Doctor of the Church who baptized St. Augustine and defended the Church's independence from the state.

celebration.name: Saint Juan Diego Cuauhtlatoatzin
celebration.quote: Am I not here, who am your Mother?
celebration.description: An indigenous Mexican layman to whom Our Lady of Guadalupe appeared, leaving her miraculous image on his tilma.

celebration.name: Saint Damasus I, Pope
celebration.quote: I, Damasus, have dedicated these verses to the honor of the martyrs.
celebration.description: The Pope who commissioned St. Jerome to translate the Bible into Latin and worked to preserve the tombs of the martyrs.

celebration.name: Saint John of the Cross, Priest and Doctor
celebration.quote: In the evening of life, we will be judged on love alone.
celebration.description: A Carmelite mystic and poet who, with St. Teresa of Avila, reformed the order and wrote profound works on the soul's journey to God.

celebration.name: Tuesday of the 3rd week of Advent
Wednesday of the 3rd week of Advent
Thursday of the 3rd week of Advent
Friday of the 3rd week of Advent
Saturday of the 3rd week of Advent
Saint Peter Canisius, Priest and Doctor
celebration.quote: If you have too much to do, with God's help you will find time to do it all.
celebration.description: A Jesuit known as the 'Second Apostle of Germany,' he played a vital role in the Counter-Reformation.

celebration.name: Tuesday of the 4th week of Advent
Saint John of Kenty, Priest
celebration.quote: Fight all error, but do it with good humor, patience, kindness, and love.
celebration.description: A professor and priest in Poland known for his immense charity, humility, and dedication to his students and the poor.

celebration.name: Thursday of the 4th week of Advent
Saint Stephen, The First Martyr
celebration.quote: Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.
celebration.description: One of the first deacons, he became the Church's first martyr, stoned to death while forgiving his executioners.

celebration.name: Saint Thomas Becket, Bishop and Martyr
celebration.quote: We worship the Creator of the universe, and we affirm that Jesus Christ is His Son.
celebration.description: A philosopher who converted to Christianity and became its first great apologist, eventually martyred for refusing to sacrifice to idols.

celebration.name: 6th day in the Octave of Christmas
Saint Sylvester I, Pope
celebration.quote: Timothy left to me only the heritage of his faith and courage.
celebration.description: Pope during the reign of Constantine, he guided the Church through its emergence from persecution.
